raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "contrasting two vices to assert a moral hierarchy favoring reason over bodily pleasure",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "anger distances a person from reason via an inward contraction akin to grief",
    "lust produces a weaker, less rational failure than anger because it yields to pleasure",
    "moral character can be ranked by degree of 'manliness' or self-mastery",
    "sin through lust reveals a more fundamental character flaw than anger"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally coherent as a Stoic-style contrast, though reliant on culturally specific notions of masculinity and reason",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "acceptance of a reason-centered ethical framework that treats passions as diagnostic of character and values self-control over pleasure",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "literal application to contemporary ethics without historical context, or use to justify gender-normative judgments about emotional expression"
}
